title: Addition of the adaptor to RNA substrates for 3′ RACE (mapping P ends)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2h7ca","text":"Simple protocol for mapping 3′-P RNA termini by RT-PCR after the addition of a 3′ adaptor using E. coli RtcB RNA ligase.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":96,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7qn41","text":"Prepare samples, including controls, according to the aim of the experiment. Use the chart below to decide which enzyme is appropriate for pre-treatments of the RNA. For example, phosphorylating all 5′ RNA ends is useful when using RtcB, because it allows blocking 5′ termini against ligation by RtcB and thus only the 3′ RACE oligo becomes the substrate for the enzyme. In such a case, use T4 polynucleotide kinase without 3′ exnuclease activity (e.g., NEB #M0236) and ATP.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":454,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"24df7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4be8","text":"Setup steps: Mix the following components (9 μL): %s %s Blocking the 3′ end of the 3′ RACE oligo is not necessary, as 3′-OH ends are not a substrate for EcRtcB RNA ligase. 5′-end has to be hydroxylated. For practical purposes, a 5′ RACE RNA oligo, which usually has both ends hydroxylated, can be used for as the 3′ RACE oligo when mapping 3′-P ends by EcRtcB.
Denature for 2 min at 70 °C, place on ice.
Purify the RNA from the RNA ligase reaction (e.g., trizol extraction or column clean-up).

Proceed to RT-PCR. For the RT reaction, use 250-500 ng of the purified adaptor-ligated RNA and a primer reverse complementary to the 3′ adaptor. For the PCR, use an upstream (forward) primer binding to the RNA of interest and a downstream (reverse) primer binding to the 3′ adaptor.
creator: Matus Valach
